content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom indices labeled. Looking at the image, I can see atom N:7 (the blue nitrogen atom) in the center-left part of the molecule.

From this detailed view, I can clearly identify the covalent bonds connected to N:7:
- C:1 (carbon with index 1) - directly bonded to N:7
- C:48 (carbon with index 48) - directly bonded to N:7  
- C:10 (carbon with index 10) - directly bonded to N:7

Wait, let me examine more carefully. Looking at the connections to N:7 (blue atom labeled "N:7"), I can see three bonds:
- One bond to C:1 (carbon, index 1)
- One bond to C:48 (carbon, index 48)
- One bond to C:10 (carbon, index 10)

All three are carbon atoms directly bonded to the nitrogen atom 7.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 9039, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_9039.xyz".

Let me confirm once more by checking if there are any hydrogen atoms bonded to N:7 that I might have missed. Looking closely at the N:7 atom, it appears to be bonded to three carbon atoms (C:1, C:48, and C:10), forming a tertiary amine structure.

Based on the visual evidence from the image, the neighbor atoms of atom N:7 are:
- Atom index 1, element C
- Atom index 10, element C  
- Atom index 48, element C

Geomconf_index: 9039

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 7, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
